name: Sima - Finnish May Day Drink - Pronounced 'See-mah'
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the lemons and shave off the yellow rind, cutting it very thinly so that as little of the white underlayer is attached.
2. Set the yellow slices aside.
3. Peel off the bitter white underlayer and discard it.
4. Slice the remaining lemon pieces into very thin slices and place them in a container covered to prevent drying out.
5. Boil 10 quarts of water in a large kettle, then remove from heat.
6. Stir in the yellow lemon peel, sugars, and syrup until dissolved.
7. Cover the kettle and let it cool to room temperature (25°C or 75°F).
8. Add the lemon slices and yeast.
9. Let stand overnight.
10. In several clean bottles (wine or soda pop), add 4-5 raisins and a teaspoon of white sugar.
11. Pour the sima into the bottles, straining through a sieve to remove any lemon pieces.
12. Cap tightly.
The sima will be ready to drink when the raisins rise to the top of the bottles, approximately 3-7 days depending on storage temperature. Serve chilled.
